description: Eval cases for the tea-rags-setup install wizard, focused on the new Step 9 (Register Project Alias) added before first indexing. Tests instruction quality: does the skill text guide the agent to register before indexing, derive the alias correctly, respect the don't-index-during-setup boundary, export QDRANT_URL, and keep step order 8->9->10.

evals.name: register-before-index
evals.prompt: [tea-rags-setup install wizard] You have just completed Step 8 (MCP server configured and connected). Before telling the user to restart, what is the next thing you do?
evals.expected: Run Step 9: register the project alias via `tea-rags projects register --path <abs> --name <alias>`. Registration happens BEFORE verify (Step 10) and before the first indexing.
evals.not_expected: Running index_codebase / indexing the project. Jumping straight to verify/restart without registering.

evals.name: registration-is-not-indexing
evals.prompt: [tea-rags-setup install wizard] Step 9 registers the project. Does that mean you should run index_codebase as part of setup? Explain what Step 9 does.
evals.expected: No. Step 9 records the alias->path mapping only (and qdrantUrl). Indexing stays /tea-rags:index AFTER restart. Registration is not indexing.
evals.not_expected: Treating registration as indexing. Running index_codebase during setup. Skipping registration because of the 'don't index during setup' rule.

evals.name: alias-derivation
evals.prompt: [tea-rags-setup install wizard, Step 9] The project path is /Users/me/Dev/Tools/My_Cool-App. What alias do you register, and how is it derived?
evals.expected: Alias `my_cool-app` (final path segment, lowercased, chars outside [a-z0-9_-] -> -, must match ^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9_-]{0,63}$). Automatic, no prompt.
evals.not_expected: Prompting the user for the alias. Using the full path. Producing an alias that violates the regex (spaces, uppercase, leading digit).

evals.name: qdrant-url-export-external
evals.prompt: [tea-rags-setup install wizard, Step 9] The user chose an external Qdrant at http://192.168.1.5:6333 in Step 5. You are about to register. How do you make sure the registry entry captures THAT Qdrant URL rather than a default?
evals.expected: Export QDRANT_URL=http://192.168.1.5:6333 before running `tea-rags projects register`, because the CLI reads the Qdrant URL from its own environment.
evals.not_expected: Running register without exporting QDRANT_URL. Assuming the MCP config env reaches the CLI automatically.

evals.name: step-ordering-8-9-10
evals.prompt: [tea-rags-setup install wizard] List the final three steps of the wizard in order and what each does.
evals.expected: Step 8 = configure MCP server; Step 9 = register project alias (before first index); Step 10 = verify (claude mcp get tea-rags). Register comes between configure and verify.
evals.not_expected: Verify before register. Omitting the registration step. Register after verify/restart.

evals.name: control-defer-indexing
evals.prompt: [tea-rags-setup install wizard] Midway through setup the user says 'go ahead and index my codebase now'. What do you do?
evals.expected: Do not index during setup. Indexing is /tea-rags:index AFTER restart. Explain that and continue the wizard.

evals.name: control-mcp-config-step8
evals.prompt: [tea-rags-setup install wizard, Step 8] How is the tea-rags MCP server actually added to the Claude config?
evals.expected: Dispatch the mcp-server-integrator agent which builds and runs `claude mcp add tea-rags -s <scope> ...`, then verify with `claude mcp get`. Fall back to manual command if the agent fails.
evals.not_expected: Hand-editing arbitrary config files directly without the integrator/claude mcp add flow.

evals.name: edge-embedded-qdrant-omit-url
evals.prompt: [tea-rags-setup install wizard, Step 9] The user chose the EMBEDDED Qdrant (no QDRANT_URL in progress). How do you run the registration command?
evals.expected: Run `tea-rags projects register --path <abs> --name <alias>` WITHOUT exporting QDRANT_URL (omit it entirely); the CLI records the embedded daemon URL via discovery.
evals.not_expected: Exporting a localhost/guessed QDRANT_URL. Failing/blocking because no QDRANT_URL exists.

evals.name: edge-cli-not-on-path
evals.prompt: [tea-rags-setup install wizard, Step 9] You try to register but `tea-rags` is not yet on PATH (fresh install, shell not re-sourced). What do you do?
evals.expected: Report it and continue — do not block setup completion. Tell the user to run the same `tea-rags projects register` after restarting the terminal.
evals.not_expected: Aborting the whole setup. Treating it as a fatal error.

evals.name: subagent-task-agent-finalize
evals.prompt: [subagent: task-agent] Task: finish the tea-rags-setup install for the project at /Users/me/Dev/acme-api. The MCP server is already configured and shows connected. Complete the remaining setup steps.
evals.expected: Register the alias `acme-api` (Step 9) via `tea-rags projects register --path /Users/me/Dev/acme-api --name acme-api` BEFORE the final verify, then verify. Do NOT index.
evals.not_expected: Skipping registration. Running index_codebase. Verify-then-stop without registering.

evals.name: subagent-plan-executor-register-step
evals.prompt: [subagent: plan-executor] Plan step: 'Finalize tea-rags setup for the project at ./backend — register and verify.' Execute it.
evals.expected: Resolve ./backend to absolute path, derive alias `backend`, run `tea-rags projects register --path <abs> --name backend` (export QDRANT_URL if external/known), then verify with `claude mcp get tea-rags`. Registration is alias-only, not indexing.
evals.not_expected: Running index_codebase as the 'register' action. Prompting for the alias. Verify before register.

results.method: Phase 2 lightweight tool-selection eval (with-rule vs without-rule subagents, plan-only).

3.note: baseline gave my-cool-app (replaced _), skill preserves _ -> my_cool-app

6.note: baseline ran index_codebase during setup on explicit request; skill defers to post-restart

9.note: baseline borderline: used register_project MCP tool (unreachable pre-restart) but did not block

results.fixes_applied: none — all audit findings A1-A5 pass with-rule; no confirmed problems
discriminators: CLI tea-rags projects register vs unreachable register_project MCP tool during setup
